# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image 
FROM node:12-alpine@sha256   # replace with your preferred base Docker Image, e.g., 'node' or any other supported version of the Alpine Linux distribution (e.g.: alpine/edge) if you want to use a specific image 
# Set working directory in container as /app and copy package*.json into it for npm install later   # replace with your preferred workdir, e.g., '/home/node' or any other path on the host machine that suits best   
WORKDIR /app    
COPY package*.json ./ 
# Install all dependencies in our app directory (e.g.: 'npm ci')      # you can use npm install instead of copy if your project has a node_modules folder already  
RUN npm ci      
# Copy the rest from source to destination for production build   
COPY . ./ 
EXPOSE 80     // replace with port number that suits best, e.g., '3001' or any other suitable value if you want a different one      # this is optional and can be removed based on your requirement  
# Run the app in production mode using npm run build (e.g.: './node_modules/.bin/ng build')    // replace with command that suits best, e.g., 'npm run prod' or any other suitable value if you want a different one      # this is optional and can be removed based on your requirement  
CMD [ "node", "server.js" ]  // change to the entry point of app (e.g.: './dist/main')    # replace with path that suits best, e.g., 'app' or any other suitable value if you want a different one      # this is optional and can be removed based on your requirement
